Petition To Suspend Powers Of Trustee Pending Trial; To Remove Trustee; To Surcharge Trustee; For An Accounting; To Determine Ownership Of Property, For Constructive Trust; To Rescind Deeds; And To Void Purported Settlement For Failure To Provide Notice And Failure To Join An Indispensable Party [Probate Code 17200(A)(B)(10), (12), 17206, 850, 9654; Code Of Civil Procedure 329]
Off Calendar May-30-2018 Continued To Jul-11-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kristofer Biorn for Petitioners.
Will Doyle for Carol Seibert (Trustee/Objector).

Mr. Biorn stated that David was not survived by anyone, that an estate has been opened and that he will take appropriate action to join the personal representative. Mr. Biorn also stated that mediation is set up with Bette Epstein and suggested they come back in October. Mr. Doyle requested an order referring the case to mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than June 29, 2018. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by July 6, 2018. The Petition to Suspend Powers of Trustee Pending Trial, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on July 11, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition To: (1) To Determine The Validity Of Trust Provisions And For Recession Of Trust Amendment; (2) For Financial Abuse Of An Elder And For Award
Off Calendar Jul-29-2015 Continued To Apr-04-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jarom Phipps for (petitioner) Nancy Smith. Bruce Feder for and with (respondent)
Carol Siebert.

The Court asked for the status of the case and if the parties were willing to mediate. Mr. Phipps stated that they are open to mediation and would like a trial date. Mr. Feder stated that they are also open to mediation, that no discovery has been done, that they would hope that mediation would be sufficient and did not believe that a trial date was necessary. Mr. Phipps stated that the decedent died in 2011 and that they would like this set for trial.
The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than October 16, 2015. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by October 19, 2015. The matter was continued to and the Parties are to return on October 26, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The matter was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial on April 4, 2016.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
